G7000x|notes_from_pm.value: TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]. Temp. Khufu. Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahsh√Ľr. Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7).
G7000x|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-2 G 7000X headword block depth. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.179 (physical p.176) opens ¬ß B. EAST FIELD with `G 7000X. TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]¬Ļ / Temp. Khufu / Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. / No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahsh√Ľr. / Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7). / REISNER and SMITH, Giza Necropolis, ii ...`. Per the chunk-2 prompt's headword-block rule, the block extends until the first bibliographic ribbon line (`REISNER and SMITH, ...`). Agent A's transcription is the longest faithful capture of this block (drops only the trailing `¬Ļ` footnote marker per the chunk-2 prompt's footnote-stripping rule); agents B and C each truncated short. Selecting A's content as the most PM-faithful capture of the full headword block.

LG84|occupant_alt_names.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-3 LG 84 PAKAP alternate-name normalisation. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.290 headword reads `LG 84. PAKAP good name WEHEBREc-EMAKHET Overseer of scribes of the King's repast, etc. Dyn. XXVI.`. The all-caps trailing-`c` (in pypdf) is PM's raised-ayin glyph for Late-Period Greek-derived throne-names like Wahibre (Apries) ‚ÄĒ same typographic class as chunk-1's `MENKAUREa` rendered with raised-`a` and resolved to `Menkaure Ņ` (U+02BF). Three agents produced three different normalisations: `Wehebrec-Emakhet` (Agent A; kept the `c` literal + capitalised `Emakhet`), `Wehebrec-emakhet` (Agent B; kept `c` + lowercase post-hyphen), `Wehebre Ņ-emakhet` (Agent C; converted `c` ‚Üí U+02BF ayin + lowercase post-hyphen). Agent C's form is the most Egyptologically-faithful: the `c` is the raised-ayin and should be the canonical ayin glyph (matches chunk-1's `Menkaure Ņ`); the post-hyphen `emakhet` is the locative `m-akhet` ("in the horizon"), not a proper name, so lowercase. Selecting Agent C.

SAQ-IputII|notes_from_pm.value: PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI.
SAQ-IputII|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-IputII headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.432 (physical p.72) prints `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT [II]¬Ļ` (regnal-number bracket + footnote marker) on a line followed by `Dyn. VI` on the next line. pypdf concatenated the lines and rendered `[II]¬Ļ` as `Il1`. Three agent forms: `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI.` (Agent A; regnal-number normalised + sentence-final period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT. Dyn. VI` (Agent B; missed the regnal number entirely and missed the period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI PYRAMID.` (Agent C; correct regnal + concatenated the next sub-heading `PYRAMID.` from the body block). Agent A is the most PM-faithful: regnal number rendered correctly per the chunk-4 bracket-regnal rule + sentence-final period preserved + headword block correctly bounded at the `Dyn. VI.` line (does NOT spill into the body's `PYRAMID.` sub-heading).

SAQ-Neit|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-Neit headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.431 (physical p.71) prints `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT` on a line followed by `Dyn. VI` on the next line, then `PYRAMID.` as a body sub-heading. Three agent forms: `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI.` (Agent A; sentence-final period preserved), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI` (Agent B; missed the period), `PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI PYRAMID.` (Agent C; concatenated the next body sub-heading). Same pattern as SAQ-IputII: Agent A is the most PM-faithful ‚ÄĒ sentence-final period preserved + headword block correctly bounded at `Dyn. VI.` line. Selecting A.

SAQ-MerenreI|notes_from_pm.value: I. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF MERENRE Ņ I. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXIX; Perring and Vyse, 8.
SAQ-MerenreI|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-4 SAQ-MerenreI headword block. PM III.2 2nd ed. 1978/1981 printed p.425 prints `MERENRńí Ņ I` (macron-ńď + raised-ayin); pypdf rendered the macron-ńď-plus-ayin cluster as `¬£c`. The conventional Egyptological transcription is `MERENRE Ņ I` (restoring the `E` vowel of the `Re` element; parallel to chunk-1's `Menkaure Ņ`). Three agent normalisations: `MERENRE Ņ I` (Agent A; restored the `E`), `MERENR Ņ I` (Agents B and C; strict-rule dropped the vowel). Selecting Agent A's form per egyptologist-reviewer F1 P1 ‚ÄĒ the `Merenre Ņ I` conventional form is the Egyptological standard. The parallel `fix_rows.py` CHUNK4_CORRECTIONS entry restores the `occupant_name` to `Merenre Ņ I` so all fields stay internally consistent. Also picks `Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. ...` (with period before PYRAMID) over Agent C's concatenated form.

G1151|notes_from_pm.value: Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu: Prophet of Re Ņ in the Sun-temple of Neuserre Ņ, etc. End of Dyn. V or later.
G1151|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1151 KEDNUFER headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.56 (physical p.53) prints `G 1151. KEDNUFER Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu: Prophet of Re Ņ in the Sun-temple of Neuserre Ņ, etc. End of Dyn. V or later.`. Three agent variants: (A) colon `Khufu:` + properly normalised ayin `Re Ņ`/`Neuserre Ņ`; (B) semicolon `Khufu;` + ayin normalised; (C) colon + un-normalised `Rea`/`Neuserrea` (raised-`a` left literal). The pypdf text-layer extraction shows COLON (verified at line 303 of chunk-6 text file), so agents A and C have the correct punctuation; the chunk-wide ayin-normalisation rule (raised-`a` ‚Üí U+02BF) means agent A's `Re Ņ`/`Neuserre Ņ` is the correct normalisation per the source-wide convention established in chunks 1 + 3 + 5 (Menkaure Ņ, Wehebre Ņ-emakhet, Userkaf-aliases etc.). Agent A's form is the only one matching BOTH the text-layer punctuation AND the source-wide ayin convention. Selecting A.

G2100a|notes_from_pm.value: King's son of his body, Greatest of the seers in On, Treasurer of the God in the ba-neteru, bar-neteru, neb-rekhyt, and dua-tawy boats, etc. Temp. Menkaure Ņ or Shepseskaf (Reisner) or early Dyn. V (Junker).
G2100a|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-7 G 2100-I-annexe (Merib's annexe to G 2100) headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 prints Menkaure Ņ with a raised-ayin glyph (parallel to chunk-1's `MENKAUREa` ‚Üí `Menkaure Ņ` ayin-normalisation). Three agent variants: (A) keeps raised-`a` literal (`Menkaurea`); (B) normalises to U+02BF (`Menkaure Ņ`) per the source-wide convention; (C) raised-`a` literal + extends with `Stone-built mastaba. LG 24.` clause. Selecting B ‚ÄĒ matches the chunks 1-6 raised-ayin normalisation discipline (U+02BF). The Stone-built mastaba / LG 24 alias from C should land in `tomb_aliases` separately, not as a notes extension.
G2100a|notes_from_pm._note: LG 24 alias not committed to tomb_aliases pending egyptologist review verification of PM's headword vs body scope.

G2156b|notes_from_pm.value: Dyn. VI. Mastaba south of tomb G 2220. There is another G 2156 east of G 2155 (see p. 79).
G2156b|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-7 G2156b (= second PM `G 2156`, Redenes; renamed from `G2156` to `G2156b` per the chunk-7 duplicate-disambiguation pre_merge rule). The Redenes G 2156 is PM's second use of the same Reisner number for a different physical mastaba (south of G 2220), with PM cross-referencing the first G 2156 (Kanenesut II, east of G 2155 on p. 79). Three agent variants on the cross-reference clause: (A) full clause with `(see p. 79)` page ref; (B) clause without page ref; (C) drops the cross-reference entirely. Agent A's form is the most PM-faithful: preserves the verbatim cross-reference page ref. Selecting A.
G2156b|notes_from_pm._note: Pre_merge mechanism for the G2156‚ÜíG2156b rename is documented in the chunk-7 transcribe.md scope note.

G1221|notes_from_pm.value: Shad (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.
G1221|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1221 SHAD headword block. The pypdf text-layer extraction (chunk-6 file line 432) reads `G 1221. SHAD (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` ‚ÄĒ the doubled `(?) (?)` is an OCR-stutter artifact, NOT what PM prints (printed-source verification: PM III.1 p.59 prints `SHAD (?)` once, per the egyptologist-reviewer pass). At merge time, before the egyptologist had verified the printed source, the three agent variants were: (A) `Shad (?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (full text-layer-verbatim form, preserving the stutter); (B) `Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (dropped the name); (C) `(?) (?), Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` (kept the hedges, dropped the name). Selecting A at merge time as the most text-layer-faithful form (captures the candidate name). The subsequent fix_rows.py CHUNK6_CORRECTIONS entry then trims to `Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.` per the egyptologist's recommended form once printed-source verification confirmed the OCR stutter. This two-step path makes the OCR/printed-source delta auditable.

G1201|notes_from_pm.value: King's son, Prophet of Heqet, heka-priest of MeŠł•yt, etc. Temp. Khufu.
G1201|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-6 G 1201 WEPEMNEFERT headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.57 (physical p.54) prints `G 1201. WEPEMNEFERT King's son, Prophet of Heqet, heka-priest of MeŠł•yt, etc. Temp. Khufu.` ‚ÄĒ only MeŠł•yt (the cobra-goddess of Thinis, transliterated mŠł•yt) gets the underdot-Šł§; Heqet (the frog-goddess, transliterated Šł•qt) appears in PM's printed text without the diacritic per the volume's typesetting convention. The pypdf text-layer renders MeŠł•yt's underdot-Šł§ glyph as a mid-word uppercase `H` (`MeHyt`), parallel to chunk-3's underdot-glyph rendering issues. Three agent variants: (A) `MeŠł•yt` (correctly normalised to underdot-Šł§); (B) `Mehyt` (dropped the underdot, lossy); (C) `MeHyt` (left the raw OCR uppercase-H, non-canonical). Agent A is the Egyptologically-faithful transcription matching PM's printed glyph. Selecting A. Pattern parallels chunk-3 LG 84's `WEHEBREc-EMAKHET` ‚Üí `Wehebre Ņ-emakhet` underdot normalisation. Egyptologist printed-source pass verified the asymmetric Heqet (no diacritic) vs MeŠł•yt (with diacritic) treatment matches PM's actual typesetting.

G2347a|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2347a notes_from_pm. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.84 (physical p.81) prints `G 2347a.` on its own line as a bare-suffix headword (no occupant name, no title cluster, no dating marker), immediately followed by sub-feature heading `Shaft C.` Per the chunk-8 prompt's Shape-2 rule, pure-numeric (or pure-suffix) bare-headword rows with no dating marker get `notes_from_pm: null`. Three agent variants: (A) `G 2347a.` (incorrectly transcribed the tomb_id line itself into notes); (B) `G 2347a. Shaft C.` (included the next sub-feature heading, out of headword scope); (C) `null` (correctly identified bare-suffix ‚Üí null per Shape-2 rule). Selecting C as the only form matching the chunk-8 prompt's documented Shape-2 normalisation.

G2381|notes_from_pm.value: Overseer of all works of the King, King's architect and builder in the Two Houses, Overseer of the royal commissions of the Pyramid of Pepy I, etc. Temp. Pepy I or Merenre Ņ I. Father (possibly), Khnementi (tomb G 2374). Stone-built mastaba. Almost completely destroyed.
G2381|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2381 MERYRE Ņ-MERYPTAŠł§ ŅANKH headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.86‚Äď87 (physical p.86‚Äď87) prints the headword `G 2381 with shaft G 2382A. MERYREa-MERYPTAHaANKH good name NEKHEBU Overseer of all works of the King, King's architect and builder in the Two Houses, Overseer of the royal commissions of the Pyramid of Pepy I, etc. Temp. Pepy I or Merenrea I.` followed by `Father (possibly), Khnementi (tomb G 2374). Stone-built mastaba. Almost completely destroyed.` Three agent variants: (A) ayin-normalised `Merenre Ņ I`, drops the `with shaft G 2382A.` structural-ID prefix (consistent with chunks 6‚Äď7 convention of placing structural identifiers in tomb_id/shared_with_tombs not notes); (B) literal raw `Merenrea I` (un-normalised raised-ayin); (C) ayin-normalised but prepends `With shaft G 2382A.` to notes. Selecting A ‚ÄĒ matches the chunk-wide ayin normalisation rule (raised-`a` ‚Üí U+02BF) AND the chunks 6‚Äď7 convention that structural shaft-attachment prefixes don't appear in notes_from_pm. The shaft G 2382A relationship is preserved structurally (parent G2381 emits the row; shaft does not).

G2381|occupant_name.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2381 occupant_name. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 prints `MERYREa-MERYPTAHaANKH` (all caps, raised-`a` glyph for ayin both times). Three agent normalisations: (A) `Meryre Ņ-Meryptah Ņankh` (capital post-hyphen `M`, NO underdot-Šł§); (B) `Meryre Ņ-meryptaŠł• Ņankh` (lowercase post-hyphen `m`, WITH underdot-Šł§); (C) `Meryre Ņ-MeryptaŠł• Ņankh` (capital post-hyphen `M`, WITH underdot-Šł§). Selecting C. Egyptological reasoning: (1) the post-hyphen element `MeryptaŠł•` is the standard transliteration of the theophoric component `mry-ptŠł•` (= 'beloved of PtaŠł•'); the underdot-Šł§ on the *ptŠł•* root is the Egyptological norm regardless of OCR-trigger rules; (2) PM's typesetting puts both halves in caps (no lowercase post-hyphen in the printed source), and chunk-7's G 7060 NEFER-MAaET ‚Üí `Nefer-Ma Ņet` precedent keeps post-hyphen elements capitalised. C's form satisfies both the Egyptological underdot convention and the chunk-7 capitalisation precedent.

G2387|occupant_name.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2387 occupant_name ‚ÄĒ same pattern as G 2381. PM III.1 prints `PEPY-MERYPTAHaANKH`. Three agent forms: (A) `Pepy-Meryptah Ņankh` (no underdot), (B) `Pepy-meryptaŠł• Ņankh` (lowercase + underdot), (C) `Pepy-MeryptaŠł• Ņankh` (capital + underdot). Selecting C for the same Egyptological + chunk-7 reasons documented in the G 2381 occupant_name override entry.

G2415|notes_from_pm.value: and wife Meti. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.
G2415|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2415 WERI / METI joint-burial notes. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.96 (physical p.91 in chunk file) prints headword `G 2415. WERI and wife METI. Late Dyn. V.` followed by `Stone-built mastaba.` Three agent variants: (A) `And wife Meti. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.` (capitalised `And` as if starting sentence after dropping `Weri`); (B) `and wife Meti. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.` (lowercase `and` preserving the original conjunction); (C) `and wife. Late Dyn. V. Stone-built mastaba.` (dropped wife name `Meti`). Selecting B ‚ÄĒ most PM-faithful: preserves wife name AND preserves PM's lowercase `and` as the continuation conjunction (the chunk-8 convention drops only the OCCUPANT NAME `Weri` itself from notes since it lives in `occupant_name`; the rest of the headword wording is preserved verbatim). Wife name `Meti` is independently captured in `co_occupants` with role `Wife`, AND `is_joint_burial: true` per the explicit two-equal-occupant headword.

G2423|notes_from_pm.value: Judge and Elder of the Hall, Secretary of every secret judgement of the Great Court, Prophet of Ma Ņet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Wife, Khenit. Stone-built mastaba.
G2423|notes_from_pm.rationale: 1/1/1 tie on chunk-8 G 2423 MEŠł§U headword block. PM III.1 2nd ed. 1974 printed p.97 (physical p.92 in chunk file) prints `G 2423. MEHU, Judge and Elder of the Hall, Secretary of every secret judgement of the Great Court, Prophet of Maaet, etc. Dyn. V-VI.` followed by `Wife, Khenit / Stone-built mastaba.` Three agent variants: (A) `... Prophet of Ma Ņet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Wife, Khenit. Stone-built mastaba.` (ayin-normalised + preserves wife clause); (B) `... Prophet of Maaet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Stone-built mastaba.` (raw OCR `Maaet` + drops wife clause); (C) `... Prophet of Ma Ņet, etc. Dyn. V-VI. Stone-built mastaba.` (ayin-normalised but drops wife clause). Selecting A ‚ÄĒ only variant satisfying BOTH (1) ayin normalisation `Maaet` ‚Üí `Ma Ņet` (chunk-wide raised-`a` ‚Üí U+02BF convention; goddess of truth/order Ma Ņet) AND (2) preservation of the wife attestation `Wife, Khenit.` which PM prints in the headword block before the `Stone-built mastaba` body cue. Wife `Khenit` is independently captured in `co_occupants` with role `Wife`.
